sourceMovie.title: The Shallows
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Shallows works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Bird Box
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Shallows clicked for you, Bird Box (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Jaws
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Shallows clicked for you, Jaws (1975) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Shallows?
faq.answer: Try Bird Box, Jaws, Eden Lake—each shares DNA with The Shallows while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Shallows?
faq.answer: The Shallows (2016) is best described as Horror, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Shallows worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
